description: Companies engaged in wholesale distribution, logistics, and supply chain management. Acts as intermediary between manufacturers and retailers/end customers. Includes wholesale distributors, 3PL providers, importers/exporters, and online fulfillment centers. Mid-market distributors typically manage 5,000-50,000 SKUs, process 100-2,000 orders per day, and have revenues of €10-100M.

processes.name: Inbound Logistics (Receive-to-Put-Away)
processes.description: Process from advance shipping notice (ASN) receipt through carrier scheduling, unloading, quality inspection, barcode/RFID scanning, and directed put-away to optimal warehouse locations. Includes cross-docking for high-velocity SKUs.

processes.name: Order Management (Order-to-Ship)
processes.description: Customer order receipt via EDI, web portal, phone, or email; order validation, credit check, available-to-promise (ATP) check, pick/pack/ship execution, carrier selection, and shipment confirmation. SLA management for same-day or next-day shipping commitments.

processes.name: Inventory Management
processes.description: Perpetual inventory tracking using FIFO/FEFO/LIFO costing, cycle counting programs, ABC-XYZ analysis, min/max replenishment triggers, slow-mover and obsolescence management, and multi-location inventory balancing.

processes.name: Supplier Replenishment (Plan-to-Purchase)
processes.description: Demand-driven replenishment using statistical forecasting (moving average, exponential smoothing), vendor-managed inventory (VMI), purchase order generation, lead time management, and supplier performance tracking.

processes.name: Returns Management (Reverse Logistics)
processes.description: Customer return authorization (RMA), return receipt and inspection, disposition decision (restock, repair, destroy, return to vendor), credit note issuance, and supplier warranty claims. Critical for e-commerce fulfillment operations.

processes.name: Accounts Payable and Supplier Invoicing
processes.description: High-volume supplier invoice processing (often 500-5,000 invoices/month), three-way PO/GR/invoice matching, dispute resolution, early payment discount capture, and supplier portal integration. GDPR-compliant vendor master management.

processes.name: Customer Billing and Collections
processes.description: Invoice generation based on shipment confirmation, customer-specific pricing and discount rules, EDI invoice transmission, credit limit monitoring, dunning process, and cash application.

processes.name: Transport Management
processes.description: Carrier selection and rate shopping, load planning and consolidation, shipment tracking, proof of delivery (POD) management, freight cost allocation, and carrier performance measurement.

kpis.name: Order Fill Rate
kpis.unit: % of order lines shipped complete on first attempt

kpis.name: On-Time Shipment Rate
kpis.unit: % of orders shipped on promised date

kpis.name: Inventory Accuracy (Cycle Count)
kpis.unit: % match between system and physical count

kpis.name: Cost per Order Line
kpis.unit: EUR per order line picked and shipped

kpis.name: Return Processing Time
kpis.unit: days from customer return receipt to credit note issuance

kpis.name: Dock-to-Stock Time
kpis.unit: hours from goods receipt to available-for-sale

pain_points: Manual processing of high-volume supplier invoices (often paper or unstructured PDF) creates bottlenecks in Accounts Payable, leading to missed early-payment discounts worth 1-3% of invoice value
Lack of automated EDI/API integration with key suppliers and customers forces manual re-keying of orders and shipping confirmations, introducing errors and delays
Paper-based proof of delivery (POD) documents get lost or delayed, causing disputes with customers and delaying cash collection by 7-14 days
Disconnected systems between WMS, ERP, and TMS create inventory discrepancies, requiring manual reconciliation during month-end closing
Inability to provide real-time shipment visibility to customers increases inbound call volume by 20-30% and erodes customer satisfaction
Manual processing of customer returns (RMA) delays credit note issuance and creates cash flow issues for customers, leading to payment disputes
Physical document storage for customs documentation, certificates of origin, and import records makes audit preparation extremely burdensome
Non-compliance with electronic invoicing mandates in target markets creates barriers to winning government and large corporate contracts
Slow approval workflows for vendor master changes and new supplier onboarding create procurement bottlenecks and supply chain delays
Fragmented pricing and contract document management leads to incorrect invoicing and costly revenue leakage (estimated 0.5-2% of revenue)
typical_systems: ERP: SAP Business One, Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central, Infor CloudSuite Distribution, Epicor Prophet 21, NetSuite
WMS (Warehouse Management System): Manhattan Associates, Blue Yonder (JDA), Körber, HighJump/Korber, SAP EWM
TMS (Transport Management System): Oracle TMS, MercuryGate, C.H. Robinson Navisphere, Transplace
EDI Platform: SPS Commerce, TrueCommerce, Cleo, OpenText GXS
Document Management: SharePoint Online, DocuWare, Laserfiche, M-Files
E-invoicing Platform: Basware, Tungsten Network (Kofax), Tradeshift, Coupa
Forecasting/Planning: Relex Solutions, Blue Yonder, Streamline, Lokad
regulatory_considerations: GDPR – Customer and employee personal data in CRM, HR systems, and shipping documents; requires data processing agreements with logistics partners
E-invoicing mandates – B2B and B2G electronic invoicing requirements vary by country (KSeF/Poland, XRechnung/Germany, FatturaPA/Italy, Chorus Pro/France)
Customs and import/export documentation – EUR.1 movement certificates, certificates of origin, customs declarations (SAD), import licenses
Food safety (if distributing food) – HACCP documentation, temperature monitoring records, lot traceability (EU Regulation 178/2002)
Pharmaceutical distribution (GDP) – Good Distribution Practice documentation, temperature-controlled storage records, qualified person oversight
Consumer product safety – CE declaration of conformity documentation, REACH substance declarations, RoHS compliance records
VAT compliance – Intra-EU trade (Intrastat reporting), reverse charge documentation, cross-border B2C VAT (OSS scheme)
Anti-bribery/corruption – Due diligence documentation for foreign business partners (EU Whistleblower Directive compliance)
